---
title: 'catalog add'
---

Add one or more entries to the catalog from build directories or archive files.

## Usage

```bash
xorq catalog add <paths...> [OPTIONS]
```

## Arguments

- **`paths`**—One or more paths. Each path is either a build directory
  (typically produced by `xorq build`) or an archive file (`.zip`).

## Options

| Option | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `-a`, `--alias` | none | Alias to assign to the added entry. Repeatable. Applied to **each** entry being added. |
| `--sync` / `--no-sync` | `--sync` | Push the catalog to its remotes after adding (when remotes are configured). |

## Examples

Add a single build directory:
```bash
xorq catalog add builds/f02d28198715
```

Add multiple builds in one invocation:
```bash
xorq catalog add builds/f02d28198715 builds/c5a981ab43cd
```

Assign aliases at add time:
```bash
xorq catalog add builds/f02d28198715 -a penguins-prod -a current
```

Add without syncing (defer the push to a later `xorq catalog push`):
```bash
xorq catalog add builds/f02d28198715 --no-sync
```

## See also

- [`catalog list`](list.qmd)—list catalog entries
- [`catalog add-alias`](add-alias.qmd)—attach an alias to an existing entry
- [`catalog remove`](remove.qmd)—remove an entry from the catalog
